summary: PR 2600 links Kirby's Donkey special translation unit by marking `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` as Matching, adding its `.sdata2` split range, converting three `.sdata2` symbols from global `ftKb_Init_*` names to local labels, adding a small `sdata2_order0` helper returning `S32_TO_F32`, and removing unnecessary callback function-pointer casts in the source file.

key_files.role: Kirby Donkey special source updated with a local helper for `.sdata2` ordering and callback assignment cleanup.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c hunk adds `static double sdata2_order0(void) { return S32_TO_F32; }`
raw_diff:src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c hunk removes casts from `death2_cb` and `take_dmg_cb` assignments

key_files.role: Build configuration changed `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` from NonMatching to Matching.
Split metadata updated to assign a `.sdata2` range to `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c`.
Symbol metadata changed three `.sdata2` entries at 0x804D9488, 0x804D9490, and 0x804D9494 from global address-style names to local labels.

what_changed.text: `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` was promoted from NonMatching to Matching in `configure.py`.
The split file now assigns `.sdata2` range `0x804D9488` through `0x804D9498` to `melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c`.

Three `.sdata2` symbols in that range were converted from global `ftKb_Init_804D9488/9490/9494` names to local labels `@222`, `@233`, and `@234` with existing object sizes and data types retained in the excerpt.
A `static double sdata2_order0(void)` helper returning `S32_TO_F32` was added, with a TODO noting it may represent an inline shared by `ftKb_DkSpecialN_Anim` and `ftKb_DkSpecialAirN_Anim`.
Two callback assignments in `ftKb_DkSpecialNStart_Coll_inline` were simplified by removing explicit casts to `void (*)(HSD_GObj*)`.

smart_moves.text: The PR kept data ownership changes narrow: only the `.sdata2` range adjacent to this TU and the three symbols inside that range were updated.
The added helper is explicitly marked as uncertain with a TODO rather than being presented as a fully understood semantic helper.
Unnecessary callback casts were removed where the assigned functions are used directly as callbacks.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a TU is ready to link as Matching, check whether nearby small constants need split and symbol metadata updates instead of compensating with misleading global names in C.
If a matching workaround is only understood as ordering or an inline artifact, preserve that uncertainty in a TODO rather than inventing a semantic name.

naming_conventions.text: The source helper name `sdata2_order0` is intentionally mechanical/order-oriented, matching the TODO's uncertainty instead of inventing a semantic name.
The `.sdata2` symbols at 0x804D9488, 0x804D9490, and 0x804D9494 were represented as local labels `@222`, `@233`, and `@234` rather than global `ftKb_Init_*` names.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Promoted the object to Matching only alongside split and symbol metadata updates for the `.sdata2` constants used by the TU.
Used a small static helper returning `S32_TO_F32` to affect or preserve `.sdata2` ordering, while documenting that its true source form is uncertain.
Removed explicit callback casts, likely relying on recovered or compatible callback types rather than forcing the assignment shape locally.

follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for `sdata2_order0` to see whether the TODO was resolved or renamed.
Search for `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` and `.sdata2 start:0x804D9488 end:0x804D9498` to verify whether this split ownership persisted.
Search for callback assignment cleanup around `death2_cb` and `take_dmg_cb` to identify whether cast removal became a broader fighter convention.
Search for symbols `@222`, `@233`, `@234` near `0x804D9488` to see whether local-label metadata remained stable.

evidence_refs: raw_diff:full diff excerpt for four changed files
metadata_json:zero issue comments, zero review comments, zero reviews
notes: The file changes are clear and the PR was merged, but there is no PR body, review discussion, CI output, objdiff output, or explicit validation log in the loaded slice.
The title and `configure.py` change support that the PR linked `ftkirbyspecialdonkey`, but the intake cannot independently claim which functions matched or what verifier commands were run.
The TODO in the source says the helper is 'some kind of inline' shared by two functions; that uncertainty should not be upgraded into a proven source-level fact.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep data split metadata aligned when promoting a TU to Matching
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2600 linked `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` by changing the object to Matching while adding the TU's `.sdata2` range and converting constants in that range from global names to local labels. This supports the reusable lesson that matching-status changes may require narrow split/symbol metadata fixes, not just source edits.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use mechanical names when only ordering behavior is known
accepted_candidate_records.text: The added `sdata2_order0` helper is paired with a TODO stating its likely inline relationship is uncertain. This is a conservative naming example: the name reflects data-order mechanics instead of inventing unproven gameplay semantics.

source_update_candidates.title: `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` `.sdata2` ownership candidate
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2600 added `.sdata2 start:0x804D9488 end:0x804D9498` under `melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` in `config/GALE01/splits.txt`.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path fact for TU data ownership; curator should confirm against current splits before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: Local `.sdata2` labels for Kirby Donkey special constants
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2600 changed `.sdata2` symbols at `0x804D9488`, `0x804D9490`, and `0x804D9494` from global `ftKb_Init_804D9488`, `ftKb_Init_804D9490`, and `ftKb_Init_804D9494` to local labels `@222`, `@233`, and `@234`.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential data-symbol metadata update; curator should verify against current symbol metadata and avoid treating the PR alone as proof of permanent ownership.
